Option D. Zero; one.

clips.label: Question 6, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. Unbalanced analog. Incorrect. Not this one: "Unbalanced analog" uses a signal conductor and reference/shield and cannot regenerate discrete high/low states. Decisive rule: A digital receiver can interpret discrete states and regenerate them; analog paths can reject or reduce noise but normally reproduce the noise along with the wanted waveform. Best answer: Digital.

clips.ttsText: [clear, calm, professional instructor; measured pace; warm but precise] Duty A. Topic: Incident-light measurement. A metric project report requires the incident illumination measured at a projection screen. Which unit should the technician record? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Lumens. Option B. Nits. Option C. Kelvins. Option D. Lux.

segments.label: Context
segments.text: Duty A. Topic: Incident-light measurement.

segments.label: Question
segments.text: A metric project report requires the incident illumination measured at a projection screen. Which unit should the technician record?

segments.label: Response instruction
segments.text: Choose the single best answer.

segments.label: Option A
segments.text: Option A. Lumens.

segments.label: Option B
segments.text: Option B. Nits.

segments.label: Option C
segments.text: Option C. Kelvins.

segments.label: Option D
segments.text: Option D. Lux.

clips.label: Question 24, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. Lumens. Incorrect. Not this one: "Lumens" measure total luminous flux from a source, not incident illuminance at a surface. Decisive rule: Lux measures incident illuminance in the SI system; lumens describe luminous flux, nits describe luminance, and kelvins describe color temperature. Best answer: Lux.

segments.label: Context
segments.text: Duty B. Topic: MAC addressing.

segments.label: Question
segments.text: A CTS is reviewing mac addressing for an AV solution. Which identifier is associated with a network interface at the data-link layer and is used for local Ethernet delivery? Select the correct labeled region in the diagram.

segments.label: Response instruction
segments.text: Choose one labeled region.

segments.label: Option A
segments.text: Option A. IPv4 address.

segments.label: Option B
segments.text: Option B. Subnet mask.

segments.label: Option C
segments.text: Option C. MAC address.

segments.label: Option D
segments.text: Option D. DNS host name.

clips.label: Question 44, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. IPv4 address. Incorrect. Not this one: "IPv4 address" is a 32-bit network-layer address used for IP delivery and routing. Decisive rule: A MAC address identifies a network interface for local data-link delivery; IP addresses support network-layer routing, a subnet mask defines the local prefix, and DNS resolves names. Best answer: MAC address.
clips.ttsText: [clear, calm, professional instructor; measured pace; warm but precise] Option A. I P version four address. Incorrect. Not this one: "I P version four address" is a 32-bit network-layer address used for I P delivery and routing. Decisive rule: A MAC address identifies a network interface for local data-link delivery; I P addresses support network-layer routing, a subnet mask defines the local prefix, and DNS resolves names. Best answer: MAC address.

segments.label: Option A
segments.text: Option A. IPv4 address.

segments.label: Rationale
segments.text: Incorrect. Not this one: "IPv4 address" is a 32-bit network-layer address used for IP delivery and routing. Decisive rule: A MAC address identifies a network interface for local data-link delivery; IP addresses support network-layer routing, a subnet mask defines the local prefix, and DNS resolves names. Best answer: MAC address.

clips.label: Question 44, option B rationale
clips.text: Option B. Subnet mask. Incorrect. Not this one: "Subnet mask" separates the network prefix from the host portion of an IPv4 address. Decisive rule: A MAC address identifies a network interface for local data-link delivery; IP addresses support network-layer routing, a subnet mask defines the local prefix, and DNS resolves names. Best answer: MAC address.
clips.ttsText: [clear, calm, professional instructor; measured pace; warm but precise] Option B. Subnet mask. Incorrect. clips.label: Question 55
clips.text: Duty B. Topic: Signal Management. In common AV cabling terminology, which description best distinguishes a cable from an individual wire? Choose the single best answer. Option A. A cable is an assembly of one or more insulated conductors or fibers within an overall construction. Option B. A cable must always contain a metallic shield. Option C. A cable contains exactly one uninsulated conductor. Option D. A cable is any conductor carrying more than 50 volts.

A cable contains exactly one uninsulated conductor. Option D. A cable is any conductor carrying more than 50 volts.

clips.label: Question 55, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. A cable is an assembly of one or more insulated conductors or fibers within an overall construction. Correct. Correct: A cable is an overall assembly that can contain one or more insulated conductors or optical fibers plus jackets, fillers, shields, or strength members as required; an individual wire is a single conductor. CTS cue: identify the signal type, path, interference control, routing function, and published cable limits before choosing or testing the link.

clips.label: Question 62
clips.text: Duty C. Topic: Control Systems. A presenter should not have to operate the display, source, audio system, and room functions separately. What is the primary user-facing benefit of the control system? Choose the single best answer. Option A. It increases the number of technical decisions the presenter makes. Option B. It replaces every device's internal firmware. Option C. It removes the need to document system operation. Option D. It simplifies operation through a consistent interface and coordinated commands.
clips.ttsText: [clear, calm, professional instructor; measured pace; warm but precise] Duty C. Topic: Control Systems. A presenter should not have to operate the display, source, audio system, and room functions separately. What is the primary user-facing benefit of the control system? Choose the single best answer. Option A. It increases the number of technical decisions the presenter makes. Option B. It replaces every device's internal firmware. Option C. It removes the need to document system operation. Option D. It simplifies operation through a consistent interface and coordinated commands.

clips.label: Question 62, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. It increases the number of technical decisions the presenter makes. Incorrect. Not this one: "It increases the number of technical decisions the presenter makes" is a valid or plausible practice in this context, so it does not satisfy the stem's requested exception. Decisive rule: A control system simplifies operation by presenting appropriate controls and coordinating commands; it does not replace device firmware or eliminate documentation. Best answer: It simplifies operation through a consistent interface and coordinated commands.

clips.text: Duty B. Topic: Control Systems. A CTS is reviewing control systems for an AV solution. Which control command is most clearly a single device function rather than a programmed multi-device sequence? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Recall presentation mode by powering displays, lowering screens, and selecting inputs. Option B. Raise the program-audio level by one step. Option C. Start conference mode by dialing, routing cameras, and recalling audio processing. Option D. End the room session by stopping sources, muting audio, and running display cooldown.

clips.label: Question 64, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. Recall presentation mode by powering displays, lowering screens, and selecting inputs. Incorrect. Not this one: The choice "Recall presentation mode by powering…and selecting inputs" addresses a related concern but omits or misorders the decisive requirement in this situation. Decisive rule: Raising one audio level by one step is a single function; the other commands coordinate several actions and are therefore programs or macros. Best answer: Raise the program-audio level by one step.

clips.label: Question 94
clips.text: Duty A. Topic: Customer Needs. A CTS is reviewing customer needs for an AV solution. Why is it important for the AV professional to obtain a full set of building plans? Draw the connection from the prompt node to the best destination. Choose the best destination for the connection. Option A. To evaluate and approve plans to ensure that room elements are compatible with the AV system installation needs.. Option B. To understand the full range of room features to ensure that the AV system design takes other building systems and components into account.. Option C. To be able to use general site plans to determine the layout of the selected AV components.. Option D. Detailed building plans for the rooms in which the systems will be installed are typically not required; only a general floor plan is necessary..

clips.label: Question 95, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. Only the person authorized to approve invoices. Incorrect. Not this one: "Only the person authorized to approve invoices" relies on an absolute or shortcut that skips required verification, coordination, safety, or documentation. Decisive rule: The AV team needs both the principal client contact and the stakeholder contacts required to clarify needs, gain site access, coordinate systems, and complete installation work. Best answer: The primary decision-maker plus the technical, IT, facilities, security, design, construction, and site contacts relevant to the work.

clips.label: Question 96
clips.text: Duty A. Topic: Customer Needs. A CTS is reviewing customer needs for an AV solution. How does the AV team use information about any identified constraints to the AV design and installation tasks? Choose the single best answer. Option A. To select alternative AV system components that are not affected by the identified constraints. Option B. To inform the client that these constraints must be removed prior to installation tasks. Option C. To eliminate specific tasks that may be adversely impacted by constraints. Option D. To develop a work-around plan when these constraints will affect the design or installation tasks.

clips.label: Question 98, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. PPE, such as hard hats, work boots, gloves, and safety glasses. Incorrect. Not this one: "PPE, such as hard hats, work boots, gloves, and safety glasses" is a valid or plausible practice in this context, so it does not satisfy the stem's requested exception. Decisive rule: The safety equipment that AV technicians should use when working at a site include PPE (such as hard hats, work boots, gloves, and safety glasses), approved nonconductive ladders, and fall protection. Nonslip handle tools are not typically considered required safety equipment. Best answer: Tools with nonslip handles.

clips.label: Question 116
clips.text: Duty A. Topic: Site Environment. What is the primary issue the AV designer should examine when evaluating the AV control or projection room area? Choose the single best answer. Option A. If the room location is close enough to run cabling to the AV components. Option B. That the room has a clear sightline to the presenter areas. Option C. If the room meets the required size, power, and HVAC requirements, and provides other services needed for the AV system components. Option D. If the proposed control room design and layout meet government construction standards and requirements.

clips.label: Question 120
clips.text: Duty A. Topic: Site Environment. Based on your review of the client needs, the display should consist of an HD wide-screen flat-panel display that is 4 feet high. How wide is the image displayed on this monitor? Choose the single best answer. Option A. 5 feet (1.52 meters). Option B. 6.41 feet (1.95 meters). Option C. 7.11 feet (2.17 meters). Option D. 8.1 feet (2.47 meters).
clips.ttsText: [clear, calm, professional instructor; measured pace; warm but precise] Duty A. Topic: Site Environment. Based on your review of the client needs, the display should consist of an HD wide-screen flat-panel display that is 4 feet high. How wide is the image displayed on this monitor? Choose the single best answer. Option A. 5 feet (1.52 meters). Option B. 6.41 feet (1.95 meters). Option C. 7.11 feet (2.17 meters). Option D. 8.1 feet (2.47 meters).

segments.label: Context
segments.text: Duty A. Topic: Site Environment.

segments.label: Question
segments.text: Based on your review of the client needs, the display should consist of an HD wide-screen flat-panel display that is 4 feet high. How wide is the image displayed on this monitor?

segments.label: Response instruction
segments.text: Choose the single best answer.

segments.label: Option A
segments.text: Option A. 5 feet (1.52 meters).

segments.label: Option B
segments.text: Option B. 6.41 feet (1.95 meters).

segments.label: Option C
segments.text: Option C. 7.11 feet (2.17 meters).

segments.label: Option D
segments.text: Option D. 8.1 feet (2.47 meters).

clips.label: Question 120, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. 5 feet (1.52 meters). Incorrect. Not this one: "5 feet (1.52 meters)" uses the wrong value, unit, or relationship for the stated quantities. Decisive rule: An HD display has an aspect ratio of 16:9, which is equal to multiplying the height by 1.7777 to determine the width. In this case, the screen height of 4 foot (1.2 meters) times 1.7777 results in a screen width of 7.11 feet (2.17 meters). Best answer: 7.11 feet (2.17 meters).

clips.text: Duty A. Topic: Site Environment. Your client is interested in installing a large projection screen in a front lobby area to display images of various projects for promotional purposes. What should be the primary initial concern of the AV designer regarding the projection system? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Ambient noise. Option B. Audience sightlines. Option C. Projector placement. Option D. Ambient light.

clips.label: Question 123, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. Count receptacles without checking circuit capacity. Incorrect. Not this one: "Count receptacles without checking circuit capacity" relies on an absolute or shortcut that skips required verification, coordination, safety, or documentation. Decisive rule: Power planning considers the AV load, existing and shared distribution loads, circuit capacity, receptacle availability, and coordination with the electrical design. Best answer: Calculate AV load, identify other loads on the distribution, and verify available circuits and receptacles.

clips.text: Option A. The resolution of the video display system. Incorrect. Not this one: "The resolution of the video display system" names a related term or action, but its function does not match the specific definition or decision requested here. Decisive rule: The primary initial consideration of the AV system designer when designing a video display system component of an AV system is determining how the client will use the display system. This will drive the subsequent decisions about resolutions, screen size, screen location, and so on. Best answer: How the client is going to use the video display system.

clips.ttsText: [clear, calm, professional instructor; measured pace; warm but precise] Duty A. Topic: Throw-distance ratio 0.8. A projector is 4.0 m from the screen and its throw ratio is 0.8:1. Enter the image width. Enter a numeric response in m.

segments.label: Context
segments.text: Duty A. Topic: Throw-distance ratio 0.8.

segments.label: Question
segments.text: A projector is 4.0 m from the screen and its throw ratio is 0.8:1. Enter the image width.

segments.label: Response instruction
segments.text: Enter a numeric response in m.

clips.label: Interaction rationale 135
clips.text: Work from the governing relationship or dependency, not from memory alone. Throw ratio = throw distance ÷ image width. Therefore image width = 4.0 ÷ 0.8 = 5.0 m. CTS cue: start with the viewing task and environment, then verify the complete signal path, image geometry, light, and device limits.

Specify sufficient end-to-end bandwidth for the highest required format, with appropriate margin, and check the lowest-bandwidth element. Option C. Maximize every component's bandwidth regardless of requirements. Option D. Evaluate only the source and display because intermediate devices do not affect bandwidth.

segments.label: Option A
segments.text: Option A. Minimize bandwidth at every device to reduce cost.

segments.label: Option B
segments.text: Option B. Specify sufficient end-to-end bandwidth for the highest required format, with appropriate margin, and check the lowest-bandwidth element.

segments.text: Option D. Evaluate only the source and display because intermediate devices do not affect bandwidth.

clips.label: Question 137, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. Minimize bandwidth at every device to reduce cost. Incorrect. Not this one: "Minimize bandwidth at every device to reduce cost" relies on an absolute or shortcut that skips required verification, coordination, safety, or documentation. Decisive rule: The signal path must support the required format end to end, and its capability is limited by the lowest-bandwidth device or link; appropriate design margin is useful, but indiscriminate maximization is not the objective. Best answer: Specify sufficient end-to-end bandwidth for the highest required format, with appropriate margin, and check the lowest-bandwidth element.

segments.text: How should an AV system designer determine the required frequency response of an audio system?

segments.label: Response instruction
segments.text: Choose the single best answer.

segments.label: Option A
segments.text: Option A. The designer should work to achieve a frequency response as wide as possible within the project budget..

segments.label: Option B
segments.text: Option B. The audio system output frequency response should match the frequency response of the audio source components..

segments.label: Option C
segments.text: Option C. The audio system frequency response should meet the industry standard of 20Hz to 20kHz..

segments.label: Option D
segments.text: Option D. The frequency response should be based on the type of applications the audio is intended to support..

clips.label: Question 139, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. The designer should work to achieve a frequency response as wide as possible within the project budget.. Incorrect. Not this one: The choice "The designer should work to achieve a…the project budget." addresses a related concern but omits or misorders the decisive requirement in this situation. Decisive rule: Required frequency response follows the intended application: speech-only systems can be narrower than music systems. Do not maximize bandwidth or default to 20 Hz-20 kHz unless the client requirement calls for it. Best answer: The frequency response should be based on the type of applications the audio is intended to support.

clips.ttsText: [clear, calm, professional instructor; measured pace; warm but precise] Duty A. Topic: A V Design. Which of the following audio processors would a system design specify to enhance the intelligibility of a videoconferencing system? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Echo canceler. Option B. DSP matrix mixer. Option C. Crossover. Option D. Compressor.

segments.label: Context
segments.text: Duty A. Topic: AV Design.

segments.label: Question
segments.text: Which of the following audio processors would a system design specify to enhance the intelligibility of a videoconferencing system?

segments.label: Response instruction

clips.label: Question 141
clips.text: Duty A. Topic: AV Design. What is the total impedance of a system consisting of three loudspeakers, each with 4 ohms of impedance, connected in series? Choose the single best answer. Option A. 2 ohms. Option B. 12 ohms. Option C. 4 ohms. Option D. 32 ohms.
clips.ttsText: [clear, calm, professional instructor; measured pace; warm but precise] Duty A. Topic: A V Design. What is the total impedance of a system consisting of three loudspeakers, each with 4 ohms of impedance, connected in series? Choose the single best answer. Option A. 2 ohms. Option B. 12 ohms. Option C. 4 ohms. Option D. 32 ohms.

segments.label: Context
segments.text: Duty A. Topic: AV Design.

segments.label: Question
segments.text: What is the total impedance of a system consisting of three loudspeakers, each with 4 ohms of impedance, connected in series?

segments.label: Response instruction
segments.text: Choose the single best answer.

segments.label: Option A
segments.text: Option A. 2 ohms.

segments.label: Option B
segments.text: Option B. 12 ohms.

segments.label: Option C
segments.text: Option C. 4 ohms.

segments.label: Option D
segments.text: Option D. 32 ohms.

clips.label: Question 141, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. 2 ohms. Incorrect. Not this one: "2 ohms" uses the wrong value, unit, or relationship for the stated quantities. Decisive rule: When wiring loudspeakers in a series, the impedance is additive, meaning that three loudspeakers that each have 4 ohms of impedance will result in a system of 12 ohms of impedance. Best answer: 12 ohms.

clips.label: Question 142
clips.text: Duty A. Topic: AV Design. In audio systems where the loudspeakers are located far from the amplifier, what type of loudspeaker system is typically used? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Direct-coupled system. Option B. Series/parallel wired loudspeaker system. Option C. Constant-voltage system that uses transformers. Option D. Low-impedance loudspeaker system.
clips.ttsText: [clear, calm, professional instructor; measured pace; warm but precise] Duty A. Topic: A V Design. In audio systems where the loudspeakers are located far from the amplifier, what type of loudspeaker system is typically used? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Direct-coupled system. Option B. Series/parallel wired loudspeaker system. Option C. Constant-voltage system that uses transformers. Option D. Low-impedance loudspeaker system.

segments.label: Context
segments.text: Duty A. Topic: AV Design.

segments.label: Question

segments.label: Response instruction

segments.label: Option A
segments.text: Option A. Direct-coupled system.

segments.label: Option B
segments.text: Option B. Series/parallel wired loudspeaker system.

clips.text: Option A. Direct-coupled system. Incorrect. Decisive rule: Audio systems where the loudspeakers are located far from the amplifier typically use a constant-voltage loudspeaker system that includes transformers for each loudspeaker. Best answer: Constant-voltage system that uses transformers.

According to the inverse square law, which formula would you use to determine the drop in acoustic energy if the user moved from 6 to 12 meters away from a sound source? Choose the single best answer. Option A. dB = 20 log(12 meters/6 meters). Option B. dB = 20 log(6 meters × 12 meters). Option C. dB = 20 log(6 meters = 12 meters). Option D. dB = 20 log(6 meters/12 meters).

clips.text: Duty B. Topic: SDI signal connector. A coaxial video cable uses a 75-ohm bayonet-lock connector for an SDI signal. Which connector is it? Choose the single best answer. Option A. XLR. Option B. BNC. Option C. 8P8C. Option D. RCA.
clips.ttsText: [clear, calm, professional instructor; measured pace; warm but precise] Duty B. Topic: SDI signal connector. A coaxial video cable uses a 75-ohm bayonet-lock connector for an SDI signal. Which connector is it? Choose the single best answer. Option A. X L R. Option B. BNC. Option C. 8P8C. Option D. RCA.

segments.label: Context
segments.text: Duty B. Topic: SDI signal connector.

segments.label: Question
segments.text: A coaxial video cable uses a 75-ohm bayonet-lock connector for an SDI signal. Which connector is it?

segments.label: Response instruction
segments.text: Choose the single best answer.

segments.label: Option A
segments.text: Option A. XLR.

segments.label: Option B
segments.text: Option B. BNC.

segments.label: Option C
segments.text: Option C. 8P8C.

segments.label: Option D
segments.text: Option D. RCA.

clips.label: Question 145, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. XLR. Incorrect. Not this one: "XLR" is commonly used for professional balanced audio, not 75-ohm SDI coax or Ethernet. Decisive rule: Professional SDI over coax commonly uses a 75-ohm BNC connector; XLR is commonly used for balanced audio, 8P8C for twisted-pair networking, and RCA for consumer unbalanced connections. Best answer: BNC.

Best answer: BNC.

clips.label: Question 150, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. Separating components according to signal type. Incorrect. Not this one: "Separating components according to signal type" names a related term or action, but its function does not match the specific definition or decision requested here. Decisive rule: Signal separation within a rack refers to grouping cables according to signal strength so that stronger signals will not create interference on cables carrying lower-level signals, such as power cables interfering with microphone cable signals. Best answer: Separating cables according to signal strength.

clips.label: Question 157
clips.text: Duty B. Topic: VLAN configuration. A CTS is reviewing vlan configuration for an AV solution. Why can manually adding an AV VLAN across an existing switched network require substantial coordination? Choose the single best answer. Option A. A VLAN always encrypts every packet. Option B. Every endpoint must use a public IP address. Option C. VLANs require replacing copper cable with fiber. Option D. The VLAN, access ports, trunks, addressing, security policy, and switch configurations must remain consistent across the affected network.

Every endpoint must use a public I P address. Option C. VLANs require replacing copper cable with fiber. Option D. The VLAN, access ports, trunks, addressing, security policy, and switch configurations must remain consistent across the affected network.

segments.label: Option B

segments.label: Option C

clips.label: Question 157, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. A VLAN always encrypts every packet. Incorrect. Not this one: "A VLAN always encrypts every packet" relies on an absolute or shortcut that skips required verification, coordination, safety, or documentation. Decisive rule: A VLAN deployment can touch multiple switches, trunks, access ports, addressing plans, and security controls, so it must be coordinated and configured consistently with the client's network team. Best answer: The VLAN, access ports, trunks, addressing, security policy, and switch configurations must remain consistent across the affected network.

clips.label: Question 161
clips.text: Duty C. Topic: Operations. In the context of AV system installations, what is the systems performance verification, or commissioning, process? Choose the single best answer. Option A. A process for registering the ownership of components. Option B. A process for documenting that the AV system conforms with international standards. Option C. A formal process for testing the elements of the AV system to ensure that they operate as intended. Option D. Officially "launching" an AV system with users within the client organization.

Step 1: Document the discrepancy and seek clarification. Step 2: Assess scope, cost, schedule, and technical impact. Step 3: Obtain written authorization through the required change process. Step 4: Update the controlled project documents and communicate the change.

Step 4: Update the controlled project documents and communicate the change.

clips.label: Question 172
clips.text: Duty B. Topic: Project Management. A CTS is reviewing project management for an AV solution. Which statement best describes the relationship of the AV installation team with other vendors on the site? Choose the single best answer. Option A. The AV team should focus on the AV installation; the project manager is responsible for coordinating work at the project.. Option B. The AV team should review the project schedule; it defines all coordination necessary for the project.. Option C. The AV team should actively communicate and coordinate with the project manager and other vendors to ensure that the AV installation is not impacted by other vendors working on the project.. Option D. The AV team should negotiate work schedules directly with the other vendors..

clips.label: Question 175
clips.text: Duty D. Topic: Maintaining Operations. A CTS is reviewing maintaining operations for an AV solution. Which of the following statements describes the typical relationship of an AV company with its suppliers and vendors? Choose the single best answer. Option A. The AV company should identify the suppliers and vendors with the lowest current prices.. Option B. The suppliers and vendors often become long-term partners with the AV company in meeting the client needs.. Option C. The AV company should work with only one supplier or vendor to procure the needed goods and services.. Option D. The AV company client will usually specify the vendors or suppliers to support the client's project..

Best answer: The suppliers and vendors often become long-term partners with the AV company in meeting the client needs.

clips.label: Question 176
clips.text: Duty D. Topic: Maintaining Operations. A CTS is reviewing maintaining operations for an AV solution. Which of the following statements best describes how an AV manager should manage stock and inventory? Select the correct labeled region in the diagram. Choose one labeled region. Option A. Check in orders as they arrive.. Option B. Ensure the proper amount of warehouse space is available for inventory.. Option C. Keep track of the equipment and supplies the company has on hand, what is on order, and what is at the client site.. Option D. Ensure that the total value of stock and inventory does not exceed a specific amount..

clips.label: Question 176, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. Check in orders as they arrive.. Incorrect. Not this one: "Check in orders as they arrive." names a related term or action, but its function does not match the specific definition or decision requested here. Decisive rule: Inventory control must show what is on hand, what is on order and due, and what has been issued to each client site; receiving or warehouse capacity alone is incomplete. Best answer: Keep track of the equipment and supplies the company has on hand, what is on order, and what is at the client site.

Choose the single best answer. Option A. Permanent adhesive on every fastener. Option B. Standard hand-tightened thumb screws. Option C. Unrated fasteners hidden behind tape. Option D. Rated tamper-resistant security fasteners with the service tool controlled by authorized staff.

Step 2: Coordinate access and notify affected users. Step 3: Inspect, clean, update, and service the identified equipment. Step 4: Test system performance. Step 5: Record work, findings, and follow-up needs.

clips.text: Work from the governing relationship or dependency, not from memory alone. clips.label: Question 179
clips.text: Duty D. Topic: Maintaining Operations. A CTS is reviewing maintaining operations for an AV solution. What is a key security concern when renting AV equipment? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Properly identifying the person taking possession of the equipment from the AV company. Option B. Ensuring that the renter knows how to properly operate the equipment. Option C. Ensuring that the renter knows how to properly transport the equipment. Option D. Ensuring that the renter has hired a security guard to protect the equipment once it has arrived at the site.

clips.label: Question 185
clips.text: Duty D. Topic: Maintenance Activities. A CTS is reviewing maintenance activities for an AV solution. Which of the following is an example of preventive maintenance? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Replacing system cabling that is causing static within the system. Option B. Replacing a projector bulb that is nearing the end of its operational life. Option C. Upgrading a display to a greater resolution to meet client needs. Option D. Upgrading the programming on a control system to address newly installed components.

clips.label: Question 185, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. Replacing system cabling that is causing static within the system. Incorrect. Not this one: "Replacing system cabling that is causing static within the system" names a related term or action, but its function does not match the specific definition or decision requested here. Decisive rule: The objective of preventive maintenance is to address system maintenance issues prior to a failure to ensure that the system continues to operate. In this case, replacing a projector bulb according to a schedule and prior to the failure of the bulb is preventive maintenance. Best answer: Replacing a projector bulb that is nearing the end of its operational life.

segments.label: Explanation
segments.text: An intermittent failure is difficult to reproduce because it is not present all the time. This makes it hard for the technician to identify and isolate the problem. CTS cue: distinguish one device function from a programmed sequence and verify both command and feedback paths.

clips.label: Question 189
clips.text: Duty D. Topic: Troubleshooting & Repair. A CTS is reviewing troubleshooting & repair for an AV solution. What is typically the most efficient process for localizing the faulty function within a malfunctioning AV system? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Test each of the components in the order of signal flow. Option B. Logically divide the system in half, determine which portion has the failure, and then repeat for the failed half. Option C. Test each of the components beginning at the final output, working backward. Option D. Begin with testing the major functions, and then move to the minor functions.
clips.ttsText: [clear, calm, professional instructor; measured pace; warm but precise] Duty D. Topic: Troubleshooting & Repair. A C T S is reviewing troubleshooting & repair for an A V solution. What is typically the most efficient process for localizing the faulty function within a malfunctioning A V system? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Test each of the components in the order of signal flow. Option B. Logically divide the system in half, determine which portion has the failure, and then repeat for the failed half. Option C. Test each of the components beginning at the final output, working backward. Option D. Begin with testing the major functions, and then move to the minor functions.

clips.label: Question 189, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. Test each of the components in the order of signal flow. Incorrect. Not this one: "Test each of the components in the order of signal flow" names a related term or action, but its function does not match the specific definition or decision requested here. Decisive rule: Divide-and-conquer localization tests the signal path near its midpoint, keeps the failed half, and repeats until the faulty stage is isolated; it usually requires fewer tests than checking every device in order. Best answer: Logically divide the system in half, determine which portion has the failure, and then repeat for the failed half.

clips.label: Question 190
clips.text: Duty D. Topic: Troubleshooting & Repair. When troubleshooting an AV system, should the AV technician consider user error as a source of the problem? Select the correct labeled region in the diagram. Choose one labeled region. Option A. No, because the users usually understand system operation. Option B. No, because the users are not considered part of the system that has failed. Option C. Yes, because users often do not understand how to properly operate the system and may have changed settings or performed other actions that caused the failure. Option D. Yes, because user error is typically considered the main cause of AV system failure.
clips.ttsText: [clear, calm, professional instructor; measured pace; warm but precise] Duty D. Topic: Troubleshooting & Repair. Yes, because users often do not understand how to properly operate the system and may have changed settings or performed other actions that caused the failure. Option D. Yes, because user error is typically considered the main cause of A V system failure.

Adding compression to the microphone signal chain. Option B. Keeping the microphone as close to the sound source as possible. Option C. Keeping the loudspeakers in front of and as far from the microphones as possible. Option D. Turning down or muting all unused microphones.

Turning down or muting all unused microphones.

segments.text: Option A. Adding compression to the microphone signal chain.

segments.label: Option B
segments.text: Option B. Keeping the microphone as close to the sound source as possible.

clips.label: Question 194, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. Adding compression to the microphone signal chain. Correct. Correct: Compression does not remove the acoustic loop that causes feedback. Improve gain-before-feedback by moving the microphone closer to the source, keeping loudspeakers in front of and away from microphones, and muting unused microphones. CTS cue: follow the signal flow and keep acoustic, electrical, and level concepts distinct.

clips.label: Question 195, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. Insufficient compression. Incorrect. Not this one: "Insufficient compression" affects dynamic-range control; it is not the usual cause of constant broadband hiss. Decisive rule: Poor gain structure is a typical source of a constant high-frequency hiss in the system. clips.text: Duty A. Topic: Display-site measurements. clips.label: Question 209, option A rationale

Decisive rule: Elevation drawings depict the characteristics of a wall using a side view, which would allow you to determine the appropriate height and location of a video display. Best answer: Elevation drawing.

clips.label: Question 211

What should the site survey record? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Only the largest height shown on the drawing. Option B. Measured heights at the relevant equipment and audience locations, the transitions, obstructions, and the drawing discrepancy. Option C. A single estimated average with no locations. Option D. The floor area because vertical conditions do not affect AV work.

clips.label: Question 221

Choose the single best answer. Option A. Specify the brightest catalog value without measuring. Option B. Assume daylight never affects perceived contrast. Option C. Reduce the source resolution. Option D. Measure representative conditions and evaluate light control, display placement, screen characteristics, contrast, and required content visibility.

clips.text: Duty A. Topic: AV Design. A designer is selecting a technically appropriate solution for the documented need. Which option identifies the total impedance of a system consisting of three loudspeakers, each with 4 ohms of impedance, connected in series? Choose the single best answer. Option A. 2 ohms. Option B. 4 ohms. Option C. 32 ohms. Option D. 12 ohms.
clips.ttsText: [clear, calm, professional instructor; measured pace; warm but precise] Duty A. Topic: A V Design. A designer is selecting a technically appropriate solution for the documented need. Which option identifies the total impedance of a system consisting of three loudspeakers, each with 4 ohms of impedance, connected in series? Choose the single best answer. Option A. 2 ohms. Option B. 4 ohms. Option C. 32 ohms. Option D. 12 ohms.

segments.label: Context
segments.text: Duty A. Topic: AV Design.

segments.label: Question
segments.text: A designer is selecting a technically appropriate solution for the documented need. Which option identifies the total impedance of a system consisting of three loudspeakers, each with 4 ohms of impedance, connected in series?

segments.label: Response instruction
segments.text: Choose the single best answer.

segments.label: Option A
segments.text: Option A. 2 ohms.

segments.label: Option B
segments.text: Option B. 4 ohms.

segments.label: Option C
segments.text: Option C. 32 ohms.

segments.label: Option D
segments.text: Option D. 12 ohms.

clips.label: Question 225, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. 2 ohms. Incorrect. Not this one: "2 ohms" uses the wrong value, unit, or relationship for the stated quantities. Decisive rule: When wiring loudspeakers in a series, the impedance is additive, meaning that three loudspeakers that each have 4 ohms of impedance will result in a system of 12 ohms of impedance. Best answer: 12 ohms.

Option A. Choose a familiar brand, then adjust the room later. Option B. Start with the available wall width only. Option C. Maximize diagonal size without checking visual acuity or sightlines. Option D. Define the content-detail task and viewing positions, assess the environment, then size and select the display to meet those requirements.

clips.label: Question 245, option A rationale
clips.text: Option A. Visible solder. Incorrect. Not this one: "Visible solder" names a related term or action, but its function does not match the specific definition or decision requested here. Decisive rule: Stray wires that extend outside the termination are a sign of an improper termination. A stray wire can cause a short that will interfere with system operation. Best answer: Visible stray wires.

clips.label: Question 248
clips.text: Duty B. Topic: Project Management. A project lead is coordinating scope, schedule, cost, documentation, and change control. Which document should an AV company submit in the event that a modification in the building design impacted the AV system installation requirements? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Modification announcement (MA). Option B. Construction change directive (CCD). Option C. Request for change (RFC). Option D. Progress report (PR).

A project lead is coordinating scope, schedule, cost, documentation, and change control. Modification announcement (MA). Option B. Construction change directive (CCD). Option C. Request for change (RFC). Option D. Progress report (PR).

clips.label: Question 249
clips.text: Duty B. Topic: Predecessor-trade delay. The millwork required before AV rack installation will be late. What should the AV project lead do? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Install the rack in an unapproved location. Option B. Report the delay only after closeout. Option C. Remove the rack from the project without authorization. Option D. Document the dependency and impact, coordinate a revised sequence with the responsible parties, and update the forecast.

Remove the rack from the project without authorization. Option D. Document the dependency and impact, coordinate a revised sequence with the responsible parties, and update the forecast.

clips.label: Question 250
clips.text: Duty B. Topic: Change-impact analysis. A requested finish change does not alter equipment quantity but delays rack access by two weeks. What belongs in the change review? Choose the single best answer. Option A. The schedule, labor, sequencing, risk, and cost effects as well as the visible scope change. Option B. Equipment quantity only. Option C. No review because the device list is unchanged. Option D. Only the client's preferred completion date.

clips.label: Question 279

Rely on memory because the crew is familiar. Option B. Record the asset identifiers, condition, authorized recipient, time, destination, and return responsibility. Option C. Track only the shipping case count. Option D. Remove serial-number labels before release.

Remove serial-number labels before release.

Choose the single best answer. Option A. The same monthly interval for every device. Option B. Only the equipment purchase date. Option C. Maintenance only after users report failure. Option D. Spray cleaner into the powered device.

Only the enclosure color. Option B. Only whether the connector can be forced into place. Option C. The replacement's retail price. Option D. Electrical and signal compatibility, firmware, licenses, addressing, configuration, control integration, and required performance.

clips.text: Option A. Only the enclosure color. Incorrect. Best answer: Electrical and signal compatibility, firmware, licenses, addressing, configuration, control integration, and required performance.

clips.label: Question 286
clips.text: Duty D. Topic: Intermittent-fault maintenance. An AV fault occurs briefly several times a week but is absent during the service visit. What is the best next step? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Collect timestamps, logs, environmental and user context, reproduce conditions where safe, and correlate the evidence before replacing parts. Option B. Replace every device in the signal path. Option C. Close the ticket because the symptom is absent. Option D. Disable monitoring to reduce alerts.

After the invoice is prepared.

What should the technician do first? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Clarify when and how the symptom occurs, review available logs, and reproduce the reported workflow before changing components. Option B. Replace the conferencing codec immediately. Option C. clips.label: Question 291
clips.text: Duty D. Topic: Troubleshooting & Repair. A technician is isolating a fault systematically before replacing components. When troubleshooting an AV system, should the AV technician consider user error as a source of the problem? Choose the single best answer. Option A. No, because the users usually understand system operation. Option B. Yes, because users often do not understand how to properly operate the system and may have changed settings or performed other actions that caused the failure. Option C. No, because the users are not considered part of the system that has failed. Option D. Yes, because user error is typically considered the main cause of AV system failure.

Choose the single best answer. Option A. No, because the users usually understand system operation. Option B. Yes, because users often do not understand how to properly operate the system and may have changed settings or performed other actions that caused the failure. Option C. No, because the users are not considered part of the system that has failed. Option D. Yes, because user error is typically considered the main cause of A V system failure.

segments.label: Option B

segments.text: Option C. Adding compression to the microphone signal chain.

segments.text: Option D. Turning down or muting all unused microphones.

clips.text: Option A. Fewer quantization levels and a lower data rate. Incorrect. Not this one: "Fewer quantization levels and a lower data rate" names a related term or action, but its function does not match the specific definition or decision requested here. Decisive rule: Each additional bit increases the number of representable amplitude levels and adds data per sample; it does not itself change sample rate or apply compression. Best answer: More quantization levels and a higher data rate.

clips.label: Question 297
clips.text: Duty D. Topic: Audio distortion. Every loudspeaker in a room sounds clean at low level, but all channels distort as normal operating level is approached. What should the technician examine first? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Loudspeaker aiming and coverage overlap. Option B. Room reverberation time only. Option C. The control-network subnet mask. Option D. Gain structure and clipping indicators at each stage.

clips.label: Question 298
clips.text: Duty D. Topic: Frozen control interface. A touch-control screen is frozen while the AV devices still operate from their local controls. Which action best isolates the fault? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Verify the touch interface's power and communication, then test or restart it using the approved procedure. Option B. Replace the controlled devices as a group. Option C. Reload firmware on every endpoint before testing the panel. Option D. Replace the network switch without checking link status.

A touch-control screen is frozen while the A V devices still operate from their local controls. Which action best isolates the fault? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Verify the touch interface's power and communication, then test or restart it using the approved procedure. Option B. Replace the controlled devices as a group. Option C. Reload firmware on every endpoint before testing the panel. Option D. Replace the network switch without checking link status.

clips.label: Question 299
clips.text: Duty D. Topic: Conference-room water leak. Water is found leaking above an energized conference-room rack. What is the first priority? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Cover the energized rack and continue operating. Option B. Keep people clear, safely isolate the electrical hazard through authorized procedures, and notify facilities or emergency personnel. Option C. Open the rack and begin drying components while power remains on. Option D. Start a component inventory before addressing the energized hazard.

Best answer: Keep people clear, safely isolate the electrical hazard through authorized procedures, and notify facilities or emergency personnel.

clips.label: Question 300
clips.text: Duty D. Topic: Projector deviation. A ceiling-mounted projector has slowly shifted out of alignment. Which maintenance response is most complete? Choose the single best answer. Option A. Apply additional digital keystone correction without inspecting the mount. Option B. Tighten the easiest visible fastener and skip image verification. Option C. Verify structural and mount security, check for vibration, realign, test the image, and document the work. Option D. Move the screen to match the shifted image without change approval.
